blocks.text: DTC P1899: Drive pulley control SOL.V. OFF stuck or driven pulley control SOL.V. ON stuck

The drive pulley control SOL.V. and driven pulley control SOL.V. control the hydraulic pressure in the hydraulic circuit. The spools in the drive pulley control SOL.V. and driven pulley control SOL.V. push valves in response to duty (load) outputs converted to current values by the PGM-FI ECU. As a result, the valve pushed by the spool releases oil pressure proportional to the current value. The PGM-FI ECU measures the current flowing in the linear solenoids of the drive pulley control SOL.V. and driven pulley control SOL.V., and feeds back control of the resulting difference compared to the command current at the duty output. The PGM-FI ECU outputs a control signal for drive/driven pulley hydraulic pressure for proper gear shifting. If the pulley ratio is overdrive when the vehicle is in gear (driving force transmission state) and the estimated input torque at start is less than the predetermined value and no belt slip occurs in the running state, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the drive pulley control SOL.V. is stuck at OFF or the driven pulley control SOL. V. and store the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
In any of the following cases.

rows: Pulley ratio greater than 0.3 and less than 0.6 for 2.5 seconds Continued from above

items.text: Drive pulley control SOL.V. OFF sticking
Driven Pulley Control SOL.V. ON sticking
Drive until the vehicle speed [vehicle speed] reaches 60 km/h.
Fully open the accelerator pedal to start the car.
Decelerate/stop the car and put the ignition in the OFF (LOCK) mode.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the D indicator light will flash without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the DTC and freeze data will be stored.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
